initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of lateral ischemia?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose lateral ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads
Presence of J-point elevation in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST-segment depression in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have lateral ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose lateral ischemia?
options: Presence of inverted T waves in lateral leads
Presence of inverted T waves in leads V1 - V4
Presence of tall, peaked T waves in lateral leads
Presence of flattened T waves in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of inverted T waves in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show inverted T waves in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have lateral ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
